mcqs.question: Which of the following is the correct sequence of steps in the Program Interrupt Cycle?

options.text: Interrupt request → Interrupt acknowledgment → Save PC → Service routine → Restore PC → Resume execution
Interrupt request → Save PC → Interrupt acknowledgment → Service routine → Restore PC → Resume execution
Save PC → Interrupt request → Interrupt acknowledgment → Service routine → Restore PC → Resume execution
Interrupt request → Save PC → Service routine → Interrupt acknowledgment → Restore PC → Resume execution

mcqs.explanation: The CPU first acknowledges the interrupt request, then saves the Program Counter (PC) and other state information before executing the ISR, ensuring the main program can be resumed correctly.

mcqs.question: Identify the key difference between a hardware interrupt and a software interrupt (trap).

options.text: Hardware interrupts are synchronous while software interrupts are asynchronous
Hardware interrupts are initiated by external I/O devices, while software interrupts are triggered by executing a specific instruction like INT or syscall
Software interrupts cannot be masked, while hardware interrupts can be masked
Hardware interrupts have higher priority than software interrupts in all architectures

mcqs.explanation: Hardware interrupts originate from external devices (keyboard, timer) asynchronously, while software interrupts are deliberate instructions (INT n, SYSENTER, SVC) executed by the CPU itself.

mcqs.question: In a vectored interrupt system, how does the CPU determine the address of the Interrupt Service Routine (ISR)?

options.text: The CPU always uses a fixed memory location for all interrupts
The interrupting device provides an interrupt vector number, which is used as an index into an Interrupt Vector Table to obtain the ISR address
The CPU polls all I/O devices sequentially to identify the source
The ISR address is stored in a special register called the Program Counter

mcqs.explanation: Vectored interrupts use an interrupt controller that provides a unique vector number for each interrupt source, which indexes into the Interrupt Vector Table to fetch the ISR starting address.

mcqs.question: Which of the following statements correctly describes the relationship between PIC (Programmable Interrupt Controller) and APIC (Advanced PIC) in x86 architecture?

options.text: APIC is used only in single-processor systems, while PIC supports multiprocessor systems
APIC extends PIC functionality by supporting interrupt priority, message-signaled interrupts, and distributed interrupt management across multiple CPUs
PIC and APIC cannot coexist in the same system
APIC has been completely replaced by PIC in modern x86 processors

mcqs.explanation: The APIC architecture was developed to overcome PIC limitations, providing support for multiprocessor systems, more interrupt lines, priority-based scheduling, and Message Signaled Interrupts (MSI).

mcqs.question: In the context of nested interrupts, what must be ensured to prevent system instability?

options.text: Nested interrupts should always be disabled to maintain simplicity
The interrupt service routine must save and restore all processor registers, and proper priority management must be implemented to prevent priority inversion
Nested interrupts are automatically handled by hardware without software intervention
Only one interrupt can be serviced at a time; nested interrupts are not supported in modern processors

mcqs.explanation: When supporting nested interrupts, the ISR must manually save processor state (PSW, registers) and implement priority checks to ensure higher-priority interrupts can preempt lower-priority ones without corrupting data.

mcqs.question: Which factor primarily determines interrupt latency in a computer system?

options.text: The clock speed of the CPU
The time taken by the interrupt controller to prioritize and acknowledge the interrupt, plus the time to save CPU state and jump to the ISR
The size of the Interrupt Vector Table
The memory access time of RAM

mcqs.explanation: Interrupt latency is the time between the interrupt request signal and the first instruction of the ISR. It depends on the interrupt controller's response time, current CPU state, and priority handling, not just CPU clock speed.

mcqs.question: In ARM Cortex-M processors, what is the function of the 'Pending Bit' in the Nested Vectored Interrupt Controller (NVIC)?

options.text: It indicates that the interrupt has been serviced and can be cleared
It allows an interrupt request to be latched even when the interrupt is masked, so it can be serviced when unmasked
It determines the priority level of the interrupt
It is used to enable the interrupt in the system

mcqs.explanation: The NVIC's pending bit latches interrupt requests that arrive when the interrupt is masked. When the interrupt is later unmasked, the pending bit ensures the latched request is serviced, preventing loss of asynchronous events.

mcqs.question: Consider a system with three interrupt sources: Timer (priority 2), Keyboard (priority 5), and Disk (priority 1), where lower numerical value indicates higher priority. If a Keyboard interrupt is being serviced and a Disk interrupt occurs, what will happen?

options.text: The Disk interrupt will be ignored because the CPU is busy executing the Keyboard ISR
The Disk interrupt will preempt the Keyboard ISR because Disk has higher priority (lower numerical value)
The Disk interrupt will wait until the Keyboard ISR completes, then be serviced
The system will crash due to priority conflict

mcqs.explanation: In a properly designed interrupt controller with priority handling, a higher-priority interrupt (Disk, priority 1) will preempt a lower-priority interrupt service (Keyboard, priority 5), suspending the current ISR to service the more urgent request.
